name: SMTP Email
description: Generic SMTP connector for sending emails from playbooks. Works with Gmail, Outlook, Microsoft 365, Exchange, and any SMTP server. Use to send alert notifications, escalation emails, and automated reports.
setup_doc:
SMTP EMAIL — SETUP GUIDE
========================

CREDENTIALS NEEDED
  Username   Your sender email address (e.g., alerts@yourcompany.com)
  Password   Your account password or app-specific password

BASE URL FORMAT
  smtp://host:port    STARTTLS on port 587  (recommended)
  smtps://host:port   SSL/TLS on port 465

COMMON SMTP SERVERS
  Gmail           smtp://smtp.gmail.com:587
  Outlook / M365  smtp://smtp.office365.com:587
  Yahoo Mail      smtp://smtp.mail.yahoo.com:587
  Exchange        smtp://mail.yourcompany.com:587
  SendGrid        smtp://smtp.sendgrid.net:587

GMAIL SETUP
  1. Enable 2-Step Verification on your Google Account
  2. Go to Google Account > Security > App Passwords
  3. Select app: Mail / device: Other
  4. Use your Gmail address as username
  5. Use the generated app password (not your login password)

OUTLOOK / MICROSOFT 365
  1. Log into https://account.microsoft.com
  2. Go to Security > Advanced Security Options
  3. Enable two-step verification if not already on
  4. Under App Passwords, generate a new password
  5. Use your full email address as username

EXCHANGE ON-PREMISES
  1. Ask your Exchange admin for the SMTP relay host
  2. Use your Windows/AD username and password
  3. Port is typically 587 (STARTTLS) or 465 (SSL)

TROUBLESHOOTING
  - 'Authentication failed': Check username/password. If MFA is
    enabled, you need an App Password, not your login password.
  - 'Connection refused': Verify host and port. Try 465 if 587 fails.
  - 'Certificate error': Use smtps:// for SSL or smtp:// for STARTTLS.
